venue.description:
Buffalo RiverWorks est un lieu unique qui offre une fusion intemporelle d’activités, de divertissements et de vues imprenables sur le front de mer. Abritant diverses ligues sportives, des parcours d’accrobranche urbains, plusieurs bars et restaurants, et même un silo à grains transformé en le plus grand du monde, RiverWorks offre un cadre unique à tous les amateurs de divertissement.

Idéalement situé sur les rives de la rivière Buffalo, ce lieu magnifique invite à l’exploration. Tout comme sur https://casino.actionclimat.ca/, vous pourrez vivre une montée d’adrénaline en participant à des activités captivantes ou vous détendre et admirer le paysage pittoresque du port.

Similaire aux sensations fortes et à l’exaltation des plateformes de jeux en ligne comme Asino, Buffalo RiverWorks déborde de vie et d’effervescence. Bien que différents par nature, les deux sites partagent le même objectif : offrir une expérience inoubliable qui vous donnera envie de revenir.

Que vous préfériez jouer au blackjack sur Asino ou faire du rafting à Buffalo RiverWorks, tous deux ont le charme et l’attrait qui vous accueilleront toujours et répondront à vos envies de divertissement et de sensations fortes. À RiverWorks, il se passe toujours quelque chose d’amusant, tout comme il y a toujours un nouveau jeu qui vous attend au Casino Action Climat. Avec un large éventail d’activités disponibles, RiverWorks s’est imposé comme un haut lieu du divertissement à Buffalo, vous offrant une expérience palpitante à chaque fois. Venez découvrir nos offres et faites de chaque visite une aventure mémorable.

🧗 About Camp

A full week of active summer programming

This Buffalo RiverWorks camp is built for kids who want to move, explore, try new activities, make friends, and come home happily exhausted. Campers ages 9-13 rotate through structured waterfront, sports, and team-building activities all week.

🪪 Ages 9-13  ·  $350 per child  ·  Lunch and snacks included

📝 Register

ℹ️  Good to Know Before You Go
▼

🧒  Who can attend camp?
▼

Kids ages 9-13 are invited to attend this week-long camp session at Buffalo RiverWorks.

🕗  What are drop-off and pick-up times?
▼

Camp programming runs 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Parent drop-off is 8:00 AM – 8:30 AM and pick-up is 4:00 PM – 4:30 PM.

🍽️  What is included?
▼

Each camper receives a camp T-shirt, daily snacks, lunch, structured full-day programming, and supervision from the Sports & Recreation Director and trained camp counselors.

▼

Yes. All attendees must complete an activity waiver before participating. Detailed camp information is mailed two weeks before the session.

🤠 About River Roundup

Country Music Nights at Buffalo RiverWorks

Giddy up! River Roundup Country Music Night comes to Buffalo RiverWorks every Thursday this summer. Thursday nights are for live country music in the beer garden — rotating bands all summer long, learn to line dance with Karen Palmeri, cold beer, waterfront views, and the best sunset in Buffalo. Free admission, no reservations needed, all ages welcome.

🪪 Free Admission · All Ages Welcome 📅 Full Schedule

ℹ️  Good to Know Before You Go
▼

🅿️  Where do I park?
▼

Parking is available in the main lot at Buffalo RiverWorks on Ganson St. Arriving early is always a good idea. Additional street parking is available nearby.

🎟️  Is admission really free?
▼

Yes! River Roundup is completely free admission. No tickets, no reservations needed — just show up and enjoy. All ages are welcome.

🚫  Is re-entry allowed?
▼

No re-entry. Once you leave the venue, you will not be permitted back in. Plan accordingly!

🎒  What’s the bag policy?
▼

Large bags and backpacks are not permitted. All personal items are subject to security search at the door. Travel light!

🚬  Smoking & vaping policy
▼

Smoking and vaping are strictly prohibited indoors per New York State law. A designated outdoor smoking area is at the rear of the building.

📸  Can I take photos or record?
▼

Photography and video are generally welcome — be respectful of other guests and the performers. Check with venue staff on the night of the event for any specific artist rules.

events.title: Death at the Silos Returns: An Electrifying Murder Mystery Dinner at Buffalo RiverWorks!
events.description:
⚡ Immersive Mystery Dinner

Death at the Silos Returns

An electrifying murder mystery dinner

Buffet dinner, live actors, clues, suspects, and prizes

📍 Buffalo RiverWorks · Silo Events Center

🎟️ Get Tickets

📅
Date
Friday, July 24
🍽️
Dinner Begins
6:00 PM
🕵️
Investigation
After Dinner
🏆
Event Ends
9:30 PM
✨ Event Highlights
Explore the historic RiverWorks grain silos as a living crime scene Immersive
Interrogate suspects, uncover clues, and submit your final guess Interactive
Buffet dinner included inside the Silo Events Center Dinner
Correctly identify the killer for a chance to win prizes Prizes

🔍 The Story

Step back into 1896

Nikola Tesla is shocking Buffalo with inventions and modern ideas. But when a reform-minded mayor is killed, suspicion spreads across the silos.

Your mission is to explore, question the suspects, gather clues, and decide who done it before the night ends.

🪪 Dinner and immersive mystery experience  ·  Light walking and standing involved

🎟️ Tickets

ℹ️  Good to Know Before You Go
▼

🅿️  Where do I park?
▼

Free parking is available in the lot on Ganson St., accessible via the Michigan St. Bridge or from the south via Ohio St.

🕐  Are event times exact?
▼

All times are subject to change. Check the event page and your registration or ticket confirmation for final details.

👨‍👩‍👧  Is this family friendly?
▼

Yes. This event is designed for RiverWorks guests and families, with details specific to the event listed above.

📍  Where is Buffalo RiverWorks?
▼

Buffalo RiverWorks is located at 359 Ganson Street in Buffalo, NY, along the Buffalo River waterfront.

events.title: Death at the Silos Returns: An Electrifying Murder Mystery Dinner at Buffalo RiverWorks!
events.description:
⚡ Immersive Mystery Dinner

Death at the Silos Returns

An electrifying murder mystery dinner

Buffet dinner, live actors, clues, suspects, and prizes

📍 Buffalo RiverWorks  ·  Silo Events Center  ·  Friday, July 31, 2026
🎟️ Get Tickets

📅
Date
Friday, July 31
🍽️
Dinner Begins
6:00 PM
🕵️
Investigation
After Dinner
🏆
Event Ends
9:30 PM
⚡ Why This Night Hits
Explore Buffalo RiverWorks as a living, breathing crime scene Immersive
Interrogate suspects, uncover clues, and decide who done it Detective Work
Experience a Tesla-inspired electric ray gun and evolving live storylines Tesla
Solve the mystery correctly for a shot at prizes Prizes

🔍 The Story

Step back into 1896

Innovation is booming, tensions are rising, and one shocking murder changes everything. After selling out every show last year, Death at the Silos is back! This immersive experience transforms Buffalo RiverWorks into a crime scene where you are part of the investigation.

Nikola Tesla is dazzling Buffalo with revolutionary ideas and dangerous new technology. But when a reform-minded mayor is mysteriously killed, suspicion spreads like wildfire. Was it Tesla himself, a corrupt Irish cop, a tipsy boat captain, or the madame running Buffalo’s most notorious house of ill repute?

🍽️ A Killer Menu

Your ticket includes both the immersive performance and a full buffet dinner inside the stunning Silo Events Center:

Honey Basil Chicken Breast (Gluten Free)
Beer Braised Pot Roast with Gravy
Six Cheese Bow Tie Pasta
Fresh Garden Salad
Smashed Red Potatoes
Seasonal Vegetable Medley
Warm Dinner Rolls
Coffee & Hot Tea (Fountain & Alcoholic beverages available for purchase)

🪪 Dinner and immersive mystery experience included  ·  Light walking and standing involved

🎟️ Get Tickets

ℹ️  Good to Know Before You Go
▼

🕵️  How does the night work?
▼

Dinner begins in the Silo Events Center. Your investigation starts the moment you walk through the doors, and after dinner guests move into the historic silos to question suspects and gather clues before returning to submit a final guess.

⚠️  What should guests expect physically?
▼

Light walking and standing are involved once the investigation moves into the silos, so guests should plan for an interactive evening rather than a seated-only show.

🍽️  Is dinner included?
▼

Yes. Your ticket includes the buffet dinner in the Silo Events Center as part of the full Death at the Silos experience.

🏆  Can you win something?
▼

Yes. Guests who correctly identify the killer can compete for prizes at the end of the night.

events.description:
🎨 Buffalo RiverWorks Summer Festival

Chalkfest 2026

Two days of large-scale chalk art, music theme inspiration, and riverfront creativity

Saturday, August 1, 2026  ·  9:00 AM – 5:00 PM
Sunday, August 2, 2026  ·  9:00 AM – 5:00 PM

📍 Buffalo RiverWorks  ·  Theme: Music  ·  Free to the public
🖍️ Chalkfest Details ↗

📅
Dates
Aug 1 & Aug 2
🎵
Theme
Music
🧑‍🎨
Artist Levels
Advanced, Youth, General
🏆
Awards
17 categories
🎨 Festival Snapshot
First come, first served placement with skill-set consideration Placement
Inside-silo premium numbered spots reserved mainly for advanced adult artists Premium Spots
Judging begins Sunday afternoon with awards at 5:00 PM Judging
Free public event alongside Wellfest on Sunday Public

🖍️ What Artists Need To Know

Chalkfest 2026 runs Saturday, August 1 and Sunday, August 2 at Buffalo RiverWorks with a Music theme. Artists can interpret the theme however they want, and signups are organized into Expert/Advanced, Expert/Advanced Youth, and General Chalker levels.

Only chalk-based removable materials are allowed. Chalk, soft pastels, washable single-color tempera base, and hair spray are permitted. Paint, crayon, ink, markers, oil pastels, or other permanent materials are not allowed.

🪪 View the full rules, signup notes, and categories on the official Chalkfest page

🎨 View Chalkfest 2026 Details

ℹ️  Good to Know Before You Go
▼

🖍️  What materials are allowed?
▼

Only removable chalk-based materials are allowed. Chalk, soft pastels, washable single-color tempera base, and hair spray are permitted. Permanent or non-washable products are not allowed.

🏅  How many award categories are there?
▼

Chalkfest 2026 includes cash prizes for the top overall placements plus trophies across 17 award categories, including youth, collaboration, originality, composition, rookie, and best-in-show honors.

⏰  When does judging happen?
▼

Judging begins Sunday afternoon, and the award ceremony is scheduled for 5:00 PM on Sunday.

👨‍👩‍👧  Is Chalkfest free for the public?
▼

Yes. Chalkfest is free to the public, and the Sunday schedule also overlaps with Wellfest.

🤠 About River Roundup

Country Music Nights at Buffalo RiverWorks

Giddy up! River Roundup Country Music Night comes to Buffalo RiverWorks every Thursday this summer. Thursday nights are for live country music in the beer garden — rotating bands all summer long, learn to line dance with Karen Palmeri, cold beer, waterfront views, and the best sunset in Buffalo. Free admission, no reservations needed, all ages welcome.

🪪 Free Admission · All Ages Welcome 📅 Full Schedule

ℹ️  Good to Know Before You Go
▼

🅿️  Where do I park?
▼

Parking is available in the main lot at Buffalo RiverWorks on Ganson St. Arriving early is always a good idea. Additional street parking is available nearby.

🎟️  Is admission really free?
▼

Yes! River Roundup is completely free admission. No tickets, no reservations needed — just show up and enjoy. All ages are welcome.

🚫  Is re-entry allowed?
▼

No re-entry. Once you leave the venue, you will not be permitted back in. Plan accordingly!

🎒  What’s the bag policy?
▼

Large bags and backpacks are not permitted. All personal items are subject to security search at the door. Travel light!

🚬  Smoking & vaping policy
▼

Smoking and vaping are strictly prohibited indoors per New York State law. A designated outdoor smoking area is at the rear of the building.

📸  Can I take photos or record?
▼

Join Mind Over Foundation Inc. and Buffalo RiverWorks on Sunday, August 16 for a full day celebrating all things pickle. Whether you’re here to compete, cheer on your favorite team, shop local vendors, or simply enjoy a pickle-themed beverage on the waterfront — PickleFest Buffalo has something for everyone.

Spectator admission is FREE. We encourage everyone to register so we can prepare for a packed day of pickle-powered fun!

▼

▼

Parking is available in the lot on Ganson St., accessible via the Michigan St. Bridge or from the south via Ohio St.

▼

▼
